title: Home Service Marketplace (Urban Company-style) — Full Multi-Panel App

requirements.text: Define the Motoko backend data schema with stable storage for all core collections: users (id, phone, name, email, role, referralCode, walletBalance, savedAddresses, isBlocked, createdAt), technicians (id, phone, name, serviceArea, assignedCategories, commissionRate, status, documentsApproved, earnings, createdAt), services (id, categoryId, name, description, price, duration, imageUrl, isActive), categories (id, name, iconUrl, order), bookings (id, customerId, technicianId, serviceId, addressId, date, timeSlot, status, notes, bookingCode, paymentMethod, totalAmount, extraCharges, invoiceUrl, createdAt), payments (id, bookingId, customerId, method, amount, status, createdAt), wallets (userId, balance, transactions[]), reviews (id, bookingId, customerId, technicianId, rating, text, createdAt), referrals (referrerId, referredUserId, rewardGranted, createdAt).

quotes: Collections: users, technicians, services, categories, bookings, payments, wallets, reviews, referrals

acceptanceCriteria: All nine collections have stable Motoko type definitions
Each record type includes all specified fields with correct types
Stable variables back each collection for upgrade persistence

requirements.text: Implement role-based access control in the Motoko backend with three roles: Customer, Technician, and Admin. All update/query endpoints must validate the caller's role before executing. Admins have full access; Technicians can only access their own jobs and earnings; Customers can only access their own bookings, wallet, and profile.
acceptanceCriteria: Role enum exists with Customer, Technician, Admin variants
Each backend function checks role before proceeding
Unauthorized calls return an explicit error variant
Admin-only functions (e.g., blockUser, approveDocument) reject non-admin callers

requirements.text: Implement user authentication endpoints in the Motoko backend: register user with phone number and role, verify OTP (simulated via a stored code), maintain session via Internet Identity principal, and logout (clear session token). Auto-login must be supported by re-checking the stored principal on subsequent calls.
acceptanceCriteria: registerUser endpoint stores user record with phone and role
verifyOTP endpoint validates code and returns session confirmation
Authenticated principal maps to a user record on every call
logout endpoint clears any stored session data for that principal

requirements.text: Implement address management endpoints: saveAddress (adds to user's savedAddresses array), setDefaultAddress (marks one address as default), listAddresses (returns all saved addresses for a user), and deleteAddress.
acceptanceCriteria: saveAddress appends a new address with a unique addressId
setDefaultAddress updates the default flag correctly
listAddresses returns the full saved-address array for the caller
deleteAddress removes the specified address

requirements.text: Implement service and category management endpoints: createCategory, listCategories, createService, updateService (price, duration, description, active flag), listServicesByCategory, getServiceDetails. Admins can create/update; all roles can read.
acceptanceCriteria: listCategories returns all active categories
listServicesByCategory filters by categoryId
getServiceDetails returns full service record including price and duration
Only Admin role can call createCategory, createService, updateService

requirements.text: Implement the booking lifecycle in the backend: createBooking (generates unique booking ID with prefix BK + timestamp + random suffix, sets status to Pending), assignTechnician (Admin or system assigns technicianId, updates status to Assigned), updateBookingStatus (accepts status transitions: Pending → Assigned → OnTheWay → InProgress → Completed | Cancelled), getBooking, listBookingsByCustomer, listBookingsByTechnician, listAllBookings (Admin).

quotes: Booking Status: Pending, Assigned, On the way, In progress, Completed, Cancelled
Connect all status changes with real-time updates

acceptanceCriteria: createBooking generates a unique booking code each time
Status transitions are validated (no skipping states arbitrarily)
assignTechnician is only callable by Admin role
listAllBookings supports filtering by status parameter
Technician can call updateBookingStatus only for their own assigned bookings

requirements.text: Implement payment and wallet endpoints: recordPayment (stores payment record linked to bookingId, supports methods: CashOnDelivery, OnlinePayment, WalletDeduction), deductFromWallet, addToWallet (for referral rewards), getWalletBalance, generateInvoice (returns a structured invoice record for a completed booking including service items, extra charges, and total).
acceptanceCriteria: recordPayment creates a payment record with method, amount, and status
deductFromWallet fails with error if balance is insufficient
generateInvoice returns all line items including extra charges added by technician
Wallet balance updates atomically with payment recording

requirements.text: Implement the technician service workflow endpoints: markReached (sets status to OnTheWay), markStarted (sets status to InProgress), addExtraItems (appends extra items like pipe, gas, spare parts with price to the booking's extraCharges list), generateFinalBill (computes base price + extra charges total), markCompleted (sets status to Completed, triggers commission deduction from technician earnings).

quotes: Add extra items (pipe, gas, spare parts)

acceptanceCriteria: Only the assigned technician can call these workflow endpoints for a booking
addExtraItems appends items and recalculates totalAmount
generateFinalBill returns base + extras sum
markCompleted updates technician earnings minus commission percentage

requirements.text: Implement earnings endpoints for technicians: getDailyEarnings(date), getWeeklyEarnings(weekStartDate), getTotalEarnings, getCommissionDeductions — all filtered to the calling technician's records.
acceptanceCriteria: getDailyEarnings filters completed bookings by calendar date
getWeeklyEarnings aggregates across 7 days from startDate
getCommissionDeductions returns list of commission entries deducted per booking
All endpoints enforce Technician role check

requirements.text: Implement the review and rating endpoints: submitReview (customerId, bookingId, technicianId, rating 1–5, text — only callable after booking is Completed), getReviewsByTechnician, getReviewsByService.
acceptanceCriteria: submitReview rejects submissions for non-Completed bookings
Duplicate review for same bookingId is rejected
getReviewsByTechnician returns all reviews with average rating computation
Rating is validated to be between 1 and 5

requirements.text: Implement the referral system: generateReferralCode (creates unique code for a user at registration), applyReferralCode (links referrer to referred user), grantReferralReward (adds wallet credit to referrer when referred user completes their first service), getReferralStats (referrer's total referrals and total rewards earned).
acceptanceCriteria: Referral codes are unique and stored on the user record
applyReferralCode stores the referral link in referrals collection
grantReferralReward only fires once per referred user's first completed booking
getReferralStats returns count and total reward amount

requirements.text: Implement Admin management endpoints: blockUser, unblockUser, addTechnician, assignServiceArea, approveDocument, suspendTechnician, reactivateTechnician, getAdminReports (daily revenue, monthly revenue, active bookings count, technician performance summary), setTechnicianCommission, createOffer, updateOffer, listOffers.
acceptanceCriteria: All admin endpoints reject calls from non-Admin principals
blockUser sets isBlocked flag preventing that user from creating bookings
getAdminReports aggregates revenue from payments collection by date range
setTechnicianCommission updates the technician's commissionRate field
createOffer and updateOffer manage the offers sub-collection in services

requirements.text: Build the Customer-facing React frontend with three main view sets: (1) Auth screens — phone number entry, OTP verification input, auto-redirect on valid session; (2) Home screen — top location bar showing selected address, search bar, auto-scrolling banner slider, service categories grid with icons, featured services horizontal scroll section, bottom navigation tabs (Home, Bookings, Rewards, Account); (3) Service screens — category service list, service detail page with image, price, duration, reviews summary, and Add to Cart button.
acceptanceCriteria: Auth flow shows phone input → OTP screen → home on success
Home screen renders location bar, search, banner slider, categories grid, and featured section
Bottom navigation switches between Home, Bookings, Rewards, Account tabs
Service list filters by selected category
Service detail page shows price, duration, reviews count, and Add to Cart CTA

requirements.text: Build the Customer booking flow screens: (1) Cart summary view with selected services; (2) Address selection screen showing saved addresses with option to add new; (3) Date & time slot picker; (4) Notes entry field; (5) Booking confirmation screen displaying unique booking ID, service summary, and selected payment method choice (Cash on Delivery, Online, Wallet).
acceptanceCriteria: Cart screen lists selected services with totals
Address screen shows saved addresses and default-marked option
Date picker prevents past dates; time slot grid shows available slots
Confirmation screen displays the generated booking ID prominently
Payment method selector shows all three options

requirements.text: Build the Customer bookings list and booking detail screens: bookings list with status badges (Pending, Assigned, On the Way, In Progress, Completed, Cancelled) filterable by status; booking detail showing technician info, live status timeline, invoice summary, and a submit review form (1–5 stars + text) that appears only for Completed bookings.

quotes: Booking Status: Pending, Assigned, On the way, In progress, Completed, Cancelled

acceptanceCriteria: Bookings list shows status chip with appropriate color for each status
Status filter tabs narrow the list
Booking detail shows a vertical status timeline
Review form is visible only when booking status is Completed and no review exists
Star rating widget accepts 1–5 selection

requirements.text: Build the Customer Rewards/Wallet screen: shows current wallet balance, transaction history list, referral code display with a share button (copies code to clipboard), and referral stats (total referred users, total rewards earned).
acceptanceCriteria: Wallet balance is displayed prominently
Transaction history shows each credit/debit with date and source
Referral code is shown in a styled card with a copy-to-clipboard button
Referral stats show count and total reward amount

requirements.text: Build the Technician-facing React frontend: (1) OTP login screen; (2) Dashboard showing list of assigned jobs with customer name, service, time slot, and Accept/Reject action buttons; (3) Active job screen with step-by-step workflow buttons: Mark Reached → Mark Started → Add Extra Items (form to add item name + price) → Generate Final Bill preview → Mark Completed; (4) Earnings screen with tabs for Daily, Weekly, and Total views and a commission deduction breakdown.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard lists all assigned jobs with Accept/Reject controls
Accepting a job navigates to the active job workflow screen
Workflow buttons appear sequentially based on current booking status
Extra items form allows adding multiple items with name and price
Final bill shows base price + extra items total
Earnings tabs switch between daily, weekly, and total aggregates

requirements.text: Build the Admin web panel React frontend with a sidebar navigation covering: Dashboard (summary cards), Users, Technicians, Bookings, Services & Categories, Offers, and Reports sections. Dashboard shows KPI cards: total bookings today, active bookings, total revenue today, and technician count.

quotes: Reports: Daily revenue, Monthly revenue, Active bookings, Technician performance

acceptanceCriteria: Sidebar navigation links to all major admin sections
Dashboard renders four KPI summary cards
All admin sections are reachable from the sidebar without page reload

requirements.text: Build Admin User Management screen: paginated table of all users with columns for name, phone, role, status, and join date. Each row has Block/Unblock toggle action. Clicking a row opens a user detail panel.
acceptanceCriteria: Users table is paginated with at least 20 rows per page
Block/Unblock button reflects current isBlocked state
Blocking a user shows confirmation dialog before proceeding
User detail panel shows bookings count and wallet balance

requirements.text: Build Admin Technician Management screen: table of all technicians with name, phone, service area, status (Active/Suspended/Pending), and documents approval state. Actions: Add Technician modal form, Approve Documents button, Assign Service Area input, Suspend/Reactivate toggle.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Technician modal collects name, phone, service area, commission rate
Approve Documents button updates documentsApproved to true
Suspend/Reactivate toggle changes technician status with confirmation
Service area is editable inline or via modal

requirements.text: Build Admin Booking Management screen: full bookings table with filter tabs by status and date range picker. Each row shows booking ID, customer, technician, service, status, and amount. Actions: manually assign technician (dropdown of available technicians), change booking status via dropdown.
acceptanceCriteria: Status filter tabs update the displayed booking list
Date range picker filters bookings by createdAt date
Assign Technician dropdown shows only active technicians
Status change dropdown validates allowed transitions

requirements.text: Build Admin Services & Categories Management screen: category list with add/edit/delete; service list per category with add/edit modal (name, description, price, duration, image URL, active toggle) and commission rate setting per service or globally per technician.
acceptanceCriteria: Category CRUD operations work without page reload
Service add/edit modal validates price (positive number) and duration (positive integer)
Commission rate field accepts percentage 0–100
Active toggle immediately reflects on service availability

requirements.text: Build Admin Reports screen with two sub-tabs: Revenue (bar chart of daily revenue for last 30 days, monthly revenue summary table) and Performance (technician performance table showing completed jobs count, average rating, total earnings, commission paid). Use a charting approach compatible with React for the bar chart.
acceptanceCriteria: Revenue bar chart renders 30 data points
Monthly revenue table groups revenue by calendar month
Technician performance table is sortable by completed jobs and earnings
Reports data is fetched from the backend via React Query

requirements.text: Apply a consistent visual design system across all three frontends (Customer, Technician, Admin): white/off-white backgrounds, blue primary color (#2563EB) with purple accent (#7C3AED), rounded corners (border-radius 12–16px on cards), soft box shadows, minimal clean typography, and card-based layout patterns. Customer and Technician views should resemble a mobile app layout (max-width 430px centered). Admin panel uses full-width desktop layout with a fixed sidebar.
acceptanceCriteria: Primary color #2563EB and accent #7C3AED are used consistently across buttons, badges, and highlights
All cards have visible border-radius and soft shadow
Customer and Technician views are contained in a max-width 430px centered container
Admin panel has a fixed left sidebar and scrollable main content area
Typography is clean with appropriate heading hierarchy

requirements.text: Implement in-app notification state management: the backend should expose a notifications endpoint that stores notification records (userId, type, message, isRead, createdAt). The frontend should poll for unread notifications and display a notification bell with unread count badge in the Customer and Technician headers. Clicking it shows a dropdown list of recent notifications. Types include: BookingConfirmation, TechnicianAssigned, ServiceCompleted, PaymentSuccess, Offer.
acceptanceCriteria: Backend stores notification records linked to userId
Frontend polls or queries notifications on screen focus via React Query
Unread count badge appears on the bell icon
Marking notification as read updates isRead flag in backend
All five notification types are representable

constraints: Do NOT use Firebase, Firestore, or any external cloud database — use Motoko stable storage on the Internet Computer backend
Do NOT use React Native or Flutter — use React with TypeScript for all three frontends (Customer, Technician, Admin) as separate route-based views
Do NOT integrate external SMS/OTP providers — simulate OTP verification within the Motoko backend
Do NOT use WebSockets or Socket.io for real-time — use React Query polling for status updates
Do NOT use external push notification services (FCM, APNs) — implement in-app notification records in the backend
Frontend immutable paths (hooks/useInternetIdentity, hooks/useActor, main.tsx, components/ui) must not be modified
All backend logic must remain in a single Motoko actor in backend/main.mo
nonGoals: Firebase, Firestore, or any third-party backend integration
Real SMS OTP delivery via Twilio or any SMS gateway
GPS / geolocation API integration (location will be entered manually)
WhatsApp share API integration (referral code share will use clipboard copy)
Stripe or any external payment gateway processing (payment method selection only)
File/document upload for technician document approval (text fields only)

required: Home service marketplace hero banner with clean illustration of a technician at work in a modern home, blue gradient background (banner-hero.dim_1200x400.png)

Service category icon set — 8 icons: Plumbing (wrench/pipe), Electrical (lightning bolt), Cleaning (broom/sparkle), AC Repair (snowflake/unit), Painting (brush/roller), Carpentry (hammer/wood), Appliance Repair (washing machine), Pest Control (bug/shield) — flat style, blue primary color on white circle background (cat-plumbing.dim_80x80.png, cat-electrical.dim_80x80.png, cat-cleaning.dim_80x80.png, cat-ac.dim_80x80.png, cat-painting.dim_80x80.png, cat-carpentry.dim_80x80.png, cat-appliance.dim_80x80.png, cat-pest.dim_80x80.png)
App logo mark — stylized house outline with a checkmark or wrench tool merged inside, blue and purple gradient, minimal flat design (app-logo.dim_256x256.png)
Promotional banner slide 1 — '50% off on first booking' offer banner, vibrant blue background, bold white text, abstract curved shapes (promo-banner-1.dim_1200x400.png)
Promotional banner slide 2 — 'Book a professional cleaner today' banner, light purple background, friendly illustration of a cleaning professional (promo-banner-2.dim_1200x400.png)
